creator: madpilot@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

Thanks for your submission.

While testing it I noticed it fails on amd64 with all supported
FreeBSD version.

Here you can find a sample tinderbox log:

http://www.madpilot.net/~mad/sdcc-3.2.0_10-CURRENT-amd64.log

Is this expected? Is the software intentionally not supporting 64 bit
machines on FreeBSD?

Also some test(if possible, unluckily I only have intel hardware) or
guess should be made about other non TIER-1 architectures.

If a fix is possible and expected while a fix is studied the port
should be marked BROKEN on amd64 stating the reason in the BROKEN
text.

If instead the project explicitly doesn't support amd64 on FreeBSD
and will probably never will it should be marked as ONLY_FOR_ARCHS
or NOT_FOR_ARCHS as appropriate.

So, can you update your patch accordingly? If you need any help
just ask :)

Thank you again for your work on FreeBSD ports.

-- 
Guido Falsi <madpilot@FreeBSD.org>
